hero.title: Master Business Communication
hero.description: The TOEIC Listening and Reading test measures the everyday English skills of people working in an international environment. This practice exam simulates the actual test conditions to help you prepare effectively.

description.heading: About this Exam
description.intro: The test is a two-hour multiple-choice assessment that consists of 200 questions divided into two sections: Listening and Reading.

listening.title: Section I: Listening
listening.body: Test takers listen to a variety of questions and short conversations recorded in English, then answer questions based on what they have heard (100 items total).

reading.title: Section II: Reading
reading.body: Test takers read a variety of materials and respond at their own pace (100 items total).

provider.heading: Provided By
provider.name: ETS Official
provider.subtitle: Educational Testing Service
provider.description: The creator of the TOEIC tests, ETS is the world's largest private nonprofit educational testing and assessment organization.

dictation.title: Dictation Practice
dictation.subtitle: Improve your listening — hear it, type it, check each sentence.
dictation.emptyHint: Come back later for new exercises!

dictationPractice.placeholder: Listen and type what you hear...

placementTest.title: English placement test

intro.title: Determine your English level
intro.description: Take a 50-question test in about 40 minutes to know your CEFR level (A1–C2) and get a tailored learning path.

placementTest.confirmSubmit: Are you sure you want to submit?

errors.profileLoad: Could not load your profile. Please refresh and try again.
errors.generate: Failed to load test. Please try again.

exitModal.title: Leave the placement test?
exitModal.body: If you exit now, your current session will be cancelled and your answers will not be saved. You'll need to start over from the beginning if you want to take the test again.

break.title: Take a break
break.confirm: Are you sure? You still have {{time}} left.

listening.audioError: Audio failed to load. Please refresh.
listening.answerAny: You can answer at any time.
listening.pressPlay: Press play to hear the question.

resultScreen.congrats: Congratulations on completing the placement test!

levelDescriptions.Pre-A1: You are just starting your English journey. You can recognize a few familiar words and simple phrases. Focus on building basic vocabulary and simple sentence patterns.
levelDescriptions.A1: You can understand and use familiar everyday expressions and very basic phrases. You can introduce yourself and ask simple personal questions.
levelDescriptions.A2: You can communicate in simple, routine tasks. You can describe your background, immediate environment, and matters of immediate need in simple terms.
levelDescriptions.B1: You can deal with most situations likely to arise while travelling. You can produce simple connected text on familiar topics and briefly give reasons and explanations for opinions and plans.
levelDescriptions.B2: You can interact with a degree of fluency and spontaneity with native speakers. You can produce clear, detailed text on a wide range of subjects and explain a viewpoint on a topical issue.
levelDescriptions.C1: You can express yourself fluently and spontaneously without much obvious searching for expressions. You can use language flexibly and effectively for social, academic, and professional purposes.
levelDescriptions.C2: You can understand with ease virtually everything heard or read. You can express yourself spontaneously, very fluently, and precisely, differentiating finer shades of meaning even in complex situations.

welcome.title: English Placement Test
welcome.meta: 50 questions · ~40 minutes · Discover your CEFR level
welcome.description: This test evaluates your Grammar, Vocabulary, Reading, and Listening skills to determine your English proficiency level (CEFR A1–C2). You'll see your result and IELTS/TOEIC equivalents as soon as you finish.
welcome.notNow: Not now, take me back to the Learning Hub

audioCheck.title: Audio check
audioCheck.subtitle: Tap play. If you can hear the sample clearly, you're good to go.
audioCheck.sampleText: Welcome to the English placement test. Please make sure you can hear this message clearly before proceeding.
audioCheck.ttsText: Welcome to the English placement test. Please make sure you can hear this message clearly before proceeding.
audioCheck.problemHint: Try: increase your volume, plug in headphones, or switch to a different browser.

brightnessCheck.title: Brightness check
brightnessCheck.subtitle: Make sure your screen is bright enough to read the test comfortably.
brightnessCheck.faintText: If you can see this text clearly, your screen brightness is set correctly for the test.
brightnessCheck.referenceText: If you can see this text clearly, your screen brightness is set correctly for the test.
brightnessCheck.problemHint: Try increasing your screen brightness in your OS or monitor settings.

rules.title: Test rules
rules.subtitle: Quick read before you begin — these matter.

items.2: Section 1 — Grammar & Vocabulary: 27 questions, 30 seconds each.
items.3: Section 2 — Reading: 11 questions, 1 minute 30 seconds each.
items.4: Section 3 — Listening: 12 questions (longer time for harder clips).
items.5: Each question auto-submits when time runs out.
items.6: You cannot go back to previous questions.
items.7: There's a break between sections (you can skip it).
items.8: Your result shows your CEFR level with IELTS/TOEIC equivalents.

rules.warning: Do not refresh the page or close the tab during the test — your progress will be lost and the session will be cancelled.
rules.agree: I have read and agree to the test rules

learningPath.title: Personalized learning path
learningPath.subtitle: Tailored to your goals and current level

header.tagline: Alicia AI uses your placement level, goals and study habits to build a roadmap that fits you — not a generic learner.

error.title: We couldn't generate your plan right now
error.description: Your inputs are saved. Try again, or edit them and retry.

generating.title: Building your personalized roadmap…
generating.subtitle: Alicia AI is reasoning across your level, goal, schedule, and preferences.

step1.title: Confirm your starting level
step1.description: Your placement test result is the baseline. You can override it if you've already improved.

noPlacement.title: No placement result on record
noPlacement.body: Take a quick placement test so Alicia can pinpoint your CEFR level. You can still continue manually below — accuracy will be limited.

levelField.hint: CEFR levels describe overall English ability — we'll personalize per skill later.

cefr.B1: Intermediate — main ideas of familiar topics
cefr.C1: Advanced — flexible & effective use of English

preview.title: Why CEFR matters
preview.description: CEFR is your baseline, but we'll combine it with your goal, schedule and learning style to truly personalize the plan.
preview.bullet1Text: Two B1 learners can have very different weak spots.
preview.bullet2Text: When skill breakdown becomes available, we'll prioritize automatically.
preview.bullet3Text: Retake placement to keep your plan calibrated.

step2.title: Set your target
step2.description: Tell Alicia what you're working toward. We'll check feasibility against your level and timeline.

targetHints.TOEFL: TOEFL iBT scores range from 0 to 120.
targetHints.CEFR: Choose a CEFR target like B2 or C1.
targetHints.General: Describe the level you want to reach.

validation.IELTS: Enter an IELTS band between 1.0 and 9.0.
validation.TOEIC: Enter a TOEIC score between 10 and 990.
validation.TOEFL: Enter a TOEFL iBT score between 0 and 120.

warning.title: Heads up — this goal may be challenging with your timeline
warning.body: You can still continue. We'll recommend a more intensive plan, or you can extend the deadline / lower the target slightly on the result screen.

preview.title: Personalization preview
preview.description: As you add details, your plan accuracy improves.

step3.title: Your study lifestyle
step3.description: We use weekly time more than the deadline itself when sizing your workload.

fields.daysHint: Pick the days you can realistically study. We'll build your weekly schedule around them.

None.label: No reminders
None.description: I'll check in on my own

preview.title: What we'll do with this
preview.description: Your schedule shapes the weekly plan. Light intensity drops mock tests; intensive packs in extra practice.
preview.intensityLine: Intensity: <0>{{intensity}}</0> — affects mock test cadence and review depth.

step4.title: Fine-tune for you
step4.description: A few last details so the plan feels like it was built for you, not a generic learner.

fields.weakest: Which skill feels weakest right now?
fields.preferences: How do you prefer to learn?
fields.preferencesHint: Pick one or more — we'll weight your roadmap accordingly.
fields.confidence: How confident are you in your English right now?

notSureCallout.title: We'll build a balanced plan
notSureCallout.body: Since you're not sure, we'll keep all skills in rotation and suggest a short diagnostic later to refine the plan.

MockOnly.label: Mock tests only
MockOnly.description: Practiced but never sat the real exam

preview.title: Ready to generate
preview.description: Alicia AI will combine everything you've told us into a roadmap with weekly milestones and recommended lessons.
preview.list4: Weekly schedule mapped to your free days
preview.list5: Real lessons, flashcards, and practice tests inside Alicia

priorities.heading: Skill priority ranking
priorities.subtitle: Your roadmap allocates time based on this ranking.

phases.heading: Roadmap phases
phases.subtitle: Three phases shape your milestone progression.

weekly.heading: Weekly schedule
weekly.subtitle: We dropped sessions onto the days you marked free.

lessons.title: Lessons & courses
lessons.description: Start here to build the skills your plan prioritizes.

flashcards.title: Flashcards to drill
flashcards.description: Short, focused decks to keep recall sharp.

quizzes.title: Quizzes & practice tests
quizzes.description: Measure progress with real Alicia assessments.

actionBar.note: You can adjust your plan any time — your inputs are saved.

discussion.title: Discussion

result.failedBody: Something went wrong. Please try again.
result.disclaimer: AI evaluation may vary ±0.5 band from official IELTS scoring

editor.promptQuote: Some people believe that remote work has improved work-life balance, while others argue it has blurred the lines between professional and personal life. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
editor.promptHint1: Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.

tips.plan: Plan your essay structure before writing.
tips.mix: Use a mix of complex and simple sentences.
tips.check: Check for spelling and grammar errors.
tips.address: Ensure you address all parts of the prompt.

timer.overtime: Over time! You should wrap up.

writing.errorOccurred: An error occurred. Please try again.
writing.task1Instruction: Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant. Write at least {{count}} words.
writing.task2Instruction: Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience. Write at least {{count}} words.

task1Tips.paraphrase: Start with a paraphrase of the prompt.
task1Tips.overview: State an overview before going into details.
task1Tips.compare: Compare key figures; don't list everything.

task2Tips.views: Present both views, then give your opinion.
task2Tips.linking: Use linking words: However, Furthermore, In contrast...
task2Tips.proofread: Proofread for spelling and grammar before submitting.

writing.placeholderTask2: In today's society, the issue of...

shell.noPassage: No passage for this question group.
